rationales.hook_strength: 'Late dinners are stealing your deep sleep' is specific and contrarian enough to pause a scroll, though 'stealing' edges toward mild hyperbole.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is one actionable idea with a clear mechanism label and concrete framing — no slide tries to do two things.
rationales.audience_fit: Tired-by-3pm, overwhelmed 20–35s will immediately recognize the late-dinner habit and the payoff (better sleep, steadier energy) is directly relevant to their daily pain.
rationales.novelty: Meal timing and circadian rhythm are well-trodden territory; the clock-not-calories reframe is the freshest angle but the individual tips (3-hour cutoff, morning light) are widely circulated.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are consistently hedged ('may help', 'many people report'), voice is plain and direct, CTA ties to actual in-app data rather than a vague promise.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential because the schedule format is actionable and the sleep hook is evergreen, but novelty ceiling limits viral sharing upside.
